free_text: Characterization of the energy distribution of neutrons
generated by 5 MeV protons on a thick beryllium target
at different emission angles.
Legnaro Laboratories(LNL) of the
Italian National Institute for Nuclear Physics(INFN)
Be target, 99.9% purity, 1.0 mm thickness was used.
Silicon-Based Recoil-Proton Spectrometer
The detection system consists of a monolithic silicon
telescope with 1 mm**2 in sensitive area coupled to
polyethylene converter having 1 mm thickness. The
telescope consist of a delta-E and a E stages,
about 2 and 500 mum in thickness, respectively.
The statistical uncertainties were calculated
through a sensitivity analysis by sampling the
corresponding experimental pulse height distributions
within their standard deviation. The contribution of
the uncertainty related to the irradiation position was
also accounted for.
.The statistical uncertainty is reported by
authors.
.Table data were received from author-
Dr.J.Esposito for fig.4,5(top) of J,ARI,69,1664,2011
